comments.author: @markdarnell614
comments.text: Saudades - pronounced in Portuguese: Sow-daudis, or in Brazilian Portuguese: Sow- Dow-sheee. Tagus River: Teh-jhoo. C'mon World Travel Doc, You can do better than THIS!

replies.author: @WorldTravelDoc
replies.text: Thank you for the helpful feedback on our pronunciations; we always strive to be more accurate in our future videos! 🇵🇹

comments.author: @markdarnell614
comments.text: ...and please tell us, Oh World Travel Doc - how "Balconies seem to hold the Memory of Everyday Life"?! Is it like "The after-taste of Wine...and the echoes of footsteps...on Stone." OMG, give me a F#cking Break! AI's clumsy attempts at Poetic Profundity are absolutely risible! Some us are more than aware of what the AI model is doing to us - taking our hard-earned acquisition of knowledge  and experiencing  - putting it in aggregate - and the spitting it back at us in a meaningless "Word Salad" that sounds like 'something', but in essence - is Hollow, and Insipid ! Have your AI model read some actual Poetry by people like the great Portuguese Poet, Fernando Pessoa, and less Hallmark Greeting Card garbage!

replies.author: @WorldTravelDoc
replies.text: Thank you for your honest feedback! 🙏 We understand that poetic narration can sometimes feel overdone or artificial — we’ll keep striving for balance and authenticity

comments.author: @markdarnell614
comments.text: World Travel Doc - You are definitely Part of the Problem!

replies.author: @WorldTravelDoc
replies.text: I’m sorry you feel that way, but I appreciate you sharing your perspective with us.

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Portugal feels like the kind of place where Europe slowly relaxes and starts enjoying life a little more. Old coastal towns, giant cliffs, and endless ocean views somehow make modern stress feel unnecessary. Honestly, even the sunsets there seem like they’re moving at a calmer and healthier pace.9:18
